data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Microsoft.com employs a microservices architecture, allowing for scalable and independent service deployment across its platform.
data.technical_spec: A suite of loosely coupled services each running a distinct process and communicating through HTTP APIs.

data.title: Cloud-Native Infrastructure
data.description: The website is hosted on Microsoft Azure cloud services, leveraging cloud-native components for resilience and scalability.
data.technical_spec: Uses Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and Azure Functions for scaling and serverless operations.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Utilizes a global CDN to ensure fast content delivery by caching web elements on servers closer to the user location.
data.technical_spec: Powered by Azure CDN with URL-based routing and caching policies.

data.title: Data Layer
data.description: Data-centric services are built on Microsoft SQL Server and Cosmos DB for structured and unstructured datasets.
data.technical_spec: Microsoft SQL Server for relational data, Cosmos DB for NoSQL and multi-model database services.

data.title: Progressive Web App (PWA) Capabilities
data.description: The site functions with PWA features like offline capabilities and push notifications to enhance mobile and desktop user experiences.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes service workers and Web App Manifest for PWA functionalities.
